blocks.html_en:
My complaint is that after this settlement nothing has materially changed except that the big labs now benefit from higher barriers to entry in their market. Authors don't make more money (other than a one time protection payment from Anthropic to publishers and some lawyers). Literally no one else benefits, except I guess used book marketplaces and book scanner vendors.

To be clear, this isn't a problem with the court process. Everything here appears perfectly in accordance with the law. It's just an absurd state to be in.

sentences.en: My complaint is that after this settlement nothing has materially changed except that the big labs now benefit from higher barriers to entry in their market.
sentences.zh: 我的抱怨是，在这次和解之后，除了大型实验室现在受益于更高的市场准入壁垒之外，没有任何实质性改变。

sentences.en: Authors don't make more money (other than a one time protection payment from Anthropic to publishers and some lawyers).
sentences.zh: 作者赚的钱并不多（除了 Anthropic 向出版商和一些律师支付的一次性保护费）。

sentences.en: Literally no one else benefits, except I guess used book marketplaces and book scanner vendors.To be clear, this isn't a problem with the court process.
sentences.zh: 从字面上看，除了我猜二手书市场和书籍扫描仪供应商之外，没有其他人受益。需要明确的是，这不是法庭程序的问题。

sentences.en: Everything here appears perfectly in accordance with the law.
sentences.zh: 这里的一切看起来都完全符合法律。

sentences.en: It's just an absurd state to be in.
sentences.zh: 这只是一种荒谬的状态。

blocks.html_en:
no we should destroy the works of these ghouls and support humans instead of this destructive and useless technology

the people operating frontier labs are bad people they cannot be trusted in any way

the best solution to them would be to send them to monster island (even though it's really a peninsula)

sentences.en: no we should destroy the works of these ghouls and support humans instead of this destructive and useless technologythe people operating frontier labs are bad people they cannot be trusted in any waythe best solution to them would be to send them to monster island (even though it's really a peninsula)

blocks.html_en:
This is such a petty and impotent ruling. If you want to ban them from using culture to make derivative works without proper compensation then do that.

But if you don't want to ban them, telling them to buy one book of each, likely second hand, is complete pettiness that resulted in destructive scanning of millions of books, many of which were already practically available in digital form.

sentences.en: This is such a petty and impotent ruling.
sentences.zh: 这是一个多么狭隘又无能的裁决。

sentences.en: If you want to ban them from using culture to make derivative works without proper compensation then do that.But if you don't want to ban them, telling them to buy one book of each, likely second hand, is complete pettiness that resulted in destructive scanning of millions of books, many of which were already practically available in digital form.
sentences.zh: 如果你想禁止他们在没有适当补偿的情况下利用文化制作衍生作品，那就这么做吧。但如果你不想禁止他们，告诉他们每本书买一本书，很可能是二手书，那就完全是小事，导致对数百万本书进行破坏性扫描，其中许多书籍实际上已经以数字形式提供。

blocks.html_en:
how much of your economic output are you comfortable with companies like Anthropic stealing to put you out of work?

at least in Player Piano they paid the workers who made the cassette tapes that made the robots work.

our current LLM overlords demand that they be able to basically steal the sum total of all human knowledge so that they can sell it back to us at a rate they set.

they should have been shunned by society and made penniless when they first announced their goals but we have a bunch of deeply misanthropic people who have money and want to make a world where computer slaves do their bidding.

sentences.en: how much of your economic output are you comfortable with companies like Anthropic stealing to put you out of work?at least in Player Piano they paid the workers who made the cassette tapes that made the robots work.our current LLM overlords demand that they be able to basically steal the sum total of all human knowledge so that they can sell it back to us at a rate they set.they should have been shunned by society and made penniless when they first announced their goals but we have a bunch of deeply misanthropic people who have money and want to make a world where computer slaves do their bidding.
sentences.zh: 你对像Anthropic这样的公司偷窃让你失业的经济产出感到满意吗？至少在Player Piano，他们付钱给那些制作让机器人工作的盒式磁带的工人。我们现在的法学硕士霸主要求他们能够基本上窃取所有人类知识的总和，这样他们就可以按照他们设定的价格将其卖回给我们。当他们第一次宣布他们的目标时，他们应该被社会回避并变得身无分文，但我们有一群极度厌恶人类的人他们有钱，想要创造一个计算机奴隶听从他们命令的世界。

blocks.html_en:
If you believe information deserves to be free, and if most of your earnings were from information that wasn't given away for free -- well, if you want people to give up their ill gotten gains, maybe you can start by setting an example.

So, mind sending me your bank account information? I'll promise to make good use of it.

sentences.en: If you believe information deserves to be free, and if most of your earnings were from information that wasn't given away for free -- well, if you want people to give up their ill gotten gains, maybe you can start by setting an example.So, mind sending me your bank account information?
sentences.zh: 如果您认为信息应该免费，并且您的大部分收入都来自非免费提供的信息，那么，如果您希望人们放弃不义之财，也许您可​​以从树立榜样开始。那么，介意向我发送您的银行帐户信息吗？

sentences.en: I'll promise to make good use of it.
sentences.zh: 我保证会好好利用它。

blocks.html_en:
You seem to think that AI companies sell you content, which is false.

You get a service. The service is using their compute power to run a model and their scientists to build the model.

sentences.en: The service is using their compute power to run a model and their scientists to build the model.
sentences.zh: 该服务正在利用他们的计算能力来运行模型，并利用他们的科学家来构建模型。

sentences.en: Did someone forget to consult with the MPAA and the RIAA on this one?
sentences.zh: 是否有人忘记就此事咨询 MPAA 和 RIAA？

sentences.en: This is a joke of an outcome. $3k per book.
sentences.zh: 这就是一个笑话的结果。每本书 3000 美元。

sentences.en: How much was it per song for Napster?
sentences.zh: Napster 每首歌多少钱？

blocks.html_en:
The RIAA typically asked for around $2-4 per song to settle without a lawsuit, which would come to a total of a few thousand because they generally only went after people sharing over a thousand songs.

In the couple of few where the party would not agree to a settlement and the RIAA sued, they would pick about 15 of the thousand+ songs to sue over. Statutory damages are a minimum of $750 per infringed work, so the total would now be about 3-5 times what their settlement offer amount had been.

Most parties then got a lawyer, the lawyer told the party that had no chance, and they would then seriously negotiate with the RIAA and get a settlement.

Only a couple would still not settle, went to trial, and did an absolutely terrible job and the judge/jury awarded well above the minimum statutory damages. The RIAA still tried to settle for well below that, but the defendants refused and kept trying to fight and did not have a happy time.

sentences.en: The RIAA typically asked for around $2-4 per song to settle without a lawsuit, which would come to a total of a few thousand because they generally only went after people sharing over a thousand songs.In the couple of few where the party would not agree to a settlement and the RIAA sued, they would pick about 15 of the thousand+ songs to sue over.
sentences.zh: RIAA 通常会要求每首歌大约 2-4 美元在不提起诉讼的情况下达成和解，总共会达到几千美元，因为他们通常只追究分享一千多首歌曲的人。在极少数当事人不同意和解而 RIAA 提起诉讼的情况下，他们会从一千多首歌曲中挑选大约 15 首进行起诉。

sentences.en: Statutory damages are a minimum of $750 per infringed work, so the total would now be about 3-5 times what their settlement offer amount had been.Most parties then got a lawyer, the lawyer told the party that had no chance, and they would then seriously negotiate with the RIAA and get a settlement.Only a couple would still not settle, went to trial, and did an absolutely terrible job and the judge/jury awarded well above the minimum statutory damages.
sentences.zh: 每件侵权作品的法定赔偿金至少为 750 美元，因此现在的赔偿总额大约是其和解金额的 3-5 倍。大多数当事人随后请了律师，律师告诉当事人没有机会，然后他们会认真与 RIAA 谈判并达成和解。只有一对夫妇仍然不和解，接受审判，做得非常糟糕，法官/陪审团判给远远高于最低法定损害赔偿金。

sentences.en: The RIAA still tried to settle for well below that, but the defendants refused and kept trying to fight and did not have a happy time.
sentences.zh: RIAA 仍试图以远低于此标准的价格解决问题，但被告拒绝并继续试图抗争，但他们并没有度过愉快的时光。

blocks.html_en:
What this does is make it harder for new AI companies to train from scratch.

Anthropic should take it from its marketing/strategic budget. It just bought itself a 1.5B moat at exactly the time it can afford it.

sentences.en: What this does is make it harder for new AI companies to train from scratch.Anthropic should take it from its marketing/strategic budget.
sentences.zh: 这样做的结果是让新的人工智能公司从头开始训练变得更加困难。Anthropic 应该从其营销/战略预算中扣除它。

sentences.en: It just bought itself a 1.5B moat at exactly the time it can afford it.
sentences.zh: 它刚刚在它能买得起的时候给自己买了一条1.5B的护城河。

blocks.html_en:
The only bad thing about OpenAI and Anthropic training on everyone's stuff without their consent is that they didn't give away the model weights afterwards.

The people who espouse copyright abolitionism believe "information wants to be (and should be) free"

So no, for these people including myself, Copyright isn't doing anything good at all. It should be abolished. None of the people in this suit should get a dime. The government should force open weight releases of all foundation models as basically the only regulation that applies to the space at this current time.

sentences.en: The only bad thing about OpenAI and Anthropic training on everyone's stuff without their consent is that they didn't give away the model weights afterwards.The people who espouse copyright abolitionism believe "information wants to be (and should be) free"So no, for these people including myself, Copyright isn't doing anything good at all.
sentences.zh: OpenAI 和 Anthropic 在未经他们同意的情况下对每个人的东西进行训练的唯一坏处是他们事后没有放弃模型权重。拥护版权废除主义的人相信“信息想要（并且应该）自由”所以不，对于包括我在内的这些人来说，版权根本没有做任何好事。

sentences.en: It should be abolished.
sentences.zh: 它应该被废除。

sentences.en: None of the people in this suit should get a dime.
sentences.zh: 这套衣服里的人都不应该得到一毛钱。

sentences.en: The government should force open weight releases of all foundation models as basically the only regulation that applies to the space at this current time.
sentences.zh: 政府应该强制所有基础模型的公开重量释放，作为目前适用于该领域的唯一法规。

blocks.html_en: This is insufficient for the human authors but seems ideal for Anthropic, who now have established a financial moat for others to train their ai on those works (except for Chinese companies, which don't care either way).

blocks.html_en:
In many ways this adds to Anthropic’s motivation to IPO.

This deal is built around Anthropic surviving. The $1.5B comes in installments, and counsel’s fees are paid in step with those installments.

The class is now effectively Anthropic’s creditor, with a direct financial interest in the company staying solvent through the payment schedule.

Civil suits compensate and the one outcome guaranteed to leave authors worse off was a verdict big enough to kill the payer.

sentences.en: In many ways this adds to Anthropic’s motivation to IPO.This deal is built around Anthropic surviving.
sentences.zh: 从很多方面来说，这都增加了 Anthropic 进行 IPO 的动力。这笔交易是围绕 Anthropic 的生存而建立的。

sentences.en: The $1.5B comes in installments, and counsel’s fees are paid in step with those installments.The class is now effectively Anthropic’s creditor, with a direct financial interest in the company staying solvent through the payment schedule.Civil suits compensate and the one outcome guaranteed to leave authors worse off was a verdict big enough to kill the payer.
sentences.zh: $1.5B 是分期付款的，律师费也与这些分期付款同步支付。该班级现在实际上是 Anthropic 的债权人，该公司的直接经济利益在整个付款计划中保持偿付能力。民事诉讼可以补偿，而一个注定会让作者处境更糟的结果是一项足以杀死付款人的判决。

sentences.zh: $1.5B 是分期付款的，律师费也与这些分期付款同步支付。该班级现在实际上是 Anthropic 的债权人，该公司的直接经济利益在整个付款计划中保持偿付能力。民事诉讼可以补偿，而一个注定会让作者处境更糟的结果是一项足以杀死付款人的判决。

blocks.html_en: In many ways this adds to Anthropic’s motivation to IPO.

sentences.en: In many ways this adds to Anthropic’s motivation to IPO.
sentences.zh: 从很多方面来说，这都增强了 Anthropic 进行 IPO 的动力。

blocks.html_en: This deal is built around Anthropic surviving. The $1.5B comes in installments, and counsel’s fees are paid in step with those installments.

sentences.en: This deal is built around Anthropic surviving.
sentences.zh: 这笔交易是围绕人类生存而建立的。

sentences.en: The $1.5B comes in installments, and counsel’s fees are paid in step with those installments.
sentences.zh: $1.5B 分期付款，律师费用与分期付款同步支付。

blocks.html_en: The class is now effectively Anthropic’s creditor, with a direct financial interest in the company staying solvent through the payment schedule.

sentences.en: The class is now effectively Anthropic’s creditor, with a direct financial interest in the company staying solvent through the payment schedule.
sentences.zh: 该阶层现在实际上是 Anthropic 的债权人，在公司中拥有直接经济利益，在整个付款计划中保持偿付能力。

blocks.html_en: Civil suits compensate and the one outcome guaranteed to leave authors worse off was a verdict big enough to kill the payer.

sentences.en: Civil suits compensate and the one outcome guaranteed to leave authors worse off was a verdict big enough to kill the payer.
sentences.zh: 民事诉讼是补偿性的，而唯一一个注定会让作者处境更糟的结果就是一项足以杀死付款人的判决。

Is anyone else having mixed feelings about AI companies' special-casing themselves being a potentially new avenue to review copyright and IP law in the first place? blocks.html_en: I support anthropics position here, on both learning from and "pirating" books. The way i see things , the publishers and authors are happy with any policy that makes them more money, and more market control, regardless of what is ethical/just/right. They would shutdown public libraries , all libraries, if they could. Aaron Swartz lost his life because he tried to make public knowledge public, and they would be happy to put every information activist to death to protect their monopolies. IMHO they have no right to stop free access on the internet. The whole copyright system is artificial and monopolistic, and the publishers are complaining yet again, that technology moves information more efficiently than they do, so they want to artificially retard it through goverment action. The real goverment action that is needed, is to protect private/personal data; not data that is actively traded commercially or publically. These tech companies are invading personal and private spaces of everyday people, and storing and training with it. Even using it for military targetting and warrantless surveillance. Anthropic is by no means a good entity, so the way to stick it to them and all tech companies, is to allow their internet scraping, but make it outright criminal to use telemetry or any surveillance techniques they have or will develop. Also... the ”creators",hollywood,publishers, have no problems scraping themselves, and lift ideas from just about everywhere they can get it. Almost every hollywood movie is just an assemblage of random memes and topical concerns of everyday ppl, distilled into embelished predictable cheese. The publishers are the original slop actors. Human Slop.

sentences.en: I support anthropics position here, on both learning from and "pirating" books.
sentences.zh: 我在这里支持人类学的立场，无论是学习书籍还是“盗版”书籍。

sentences.en: The way i see things , the publishers and authors are happy with any policy that makes them more money, and more market control, regardless of what is ethical/just/right.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，出版商和作者对任何能让他们赚更多钱、获得更多市场控制权的政策都很满意，无论什么是道德/公正/正确的。

sentences.en: They would shutdown public libraries , all libraries, if they could.
sentences.zh: 如果可以的话，他们将关闭公共图书馆，所有图书馆。

sentences.en: Aaron Swartz lost his life because he tried to make public knowledge public, and they would be happy to put every information activist to death to protect their monopolies.
sentences.zh: 亚伦·斯沃茨（Aaron Swartz）因为试图将公共知识公开而丧生，他们很乐意处死每一个信息活动人士以保护他们的垄断地位。

sentences.en: IMHO they have no right to stop free access on the internet.
sentences.zh: 恕我直言，他们无权阻止免费访问互联网。

sentences.en: The whole copyright system is artificial and monopolistic, and the publishers are complaining yet again, that technology moves information more efficiently than they do, so they want to artificially retard it through goverment action.
sentences.zh: 整个版权制度是人为的和垄断的，出版商再次抱怨，技术比他们更有效地传递信息，所以他们想通过政府行动人为地阻碍它。

sentences.en: The real goverment action that is needed, is to protect private/personal data; not data that is actively traded commercially or publically.
sentences.zh: 政府真正需要采取的行动是保护私人/个人数据；不是活跃的商业或公开交易的数据。

sentences.en: These tech companies are invading personal and private spaces of everyday people, and storing and training with it.
sentences.zh: 这些科技公司正在侵入普通人的个人和私人空间，并用它来存储和训练。

sentences.en: Even using it for military targetting and warrantless surveillance.
sentences.zh: 甚至将其用于军事目标和未经授权的监视。

sentences.en: Anthropic is by no means a good entity, so the way to stick it to them and all tech companies, is to allow their internet scraping, but make it outright criminal to use telemetry or any surveillance techniques they have or will develop.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 绝不是一个好的实体，因此将其与他们和所有科技公司粘在一起的方法是允许他们进行互联网抓取，但使用遥测或他们拥有或将开发的任何监视技术将成为彻头彻尾的犯罪行为。

sentences.en: Also...
the ”creators",hollywood,publishers, have no problems scraping themselves, and lift ideas from just about everywhere they can get it.
sentences.zh: “创造者”、好莱坞、出版商，毫无疑问地从他们能得到的任何地方汲取灵感。

sentences.en: Almost every hollywood movie is just an assemblage of random memes and topical concerns of everyday ppl, distilled into embelished predictable cheese.
sentences.zh: 几乎每一部好莱坞电影都只是随机模因和日常人们关心的话题的集合，被提炼成经过修饰的可预测的奶酪。

sentences.en: The publishers are the original slop actors.
sentences.zh: 出版商是最初的演员。

sentences.en: Human Slop.
sentences.zh: 人类斜坡。

sentences.en: These tech companies are invading personal and private spaces of everyday people, and storing and training with it.
sentences.zh: 这些科技公司正在侵入普通人的个人和私人空间，并用它来存储和训练。

sentences.en: Even using it for military targetting and warrantless surveillance.
sentences.zh: 甚至将其用于军事目标和未经授权的监视。

sentences.en: Anthropic is by no means a good entity, so the way to stick it to them and all tech companies, is to allow their internet scraping, but make it outright criminal to use telemetry or any surveillance techniques they have or will develop.
sentences.zh: Anthropic 绝不是一个好的实体，因此将其与他们和所有科技公司粘在一起的方法是允许他们进行互联网抓取，但使用遥测或他们拥有或将开发的任何监视技术将成为彻头彻尾的犯罪行为。

sentences.en: Also...
the ”creators",hollywood,publishers, have no problems scraping themselves, and lift ideas from just about everywhere they can get it.
sentences.zh: “创造者”、好莱坞、出版商，毫无疑问地从他们能得到的任何地方汲取灵感。

sentences.en: Almost every hollywood movie is just an assemblage of random memes and topical concerns of everyday ppl, distilled into embelished predictable cheese.
sentences.zh: 几乎每一部好莱坞电影都只是随机模因和日常人们关心的话题的集合，被提炼成经过修饰的可预测的奶酪。

sentences.en: The publishers are the original slop actors.
sentences.zh: 出版商是最初的演员。

sentences.en: Human Slop.
sentences.zh: 人类斜坡。

blocks.html_en: Aaron swartz lost his life because he committed suicide. Something he had tried multiple times before. If he really only committed suicide because of the legal jeopardy he was in, wouldn't it have made more sense to commit suicide after you're found guilty?

sentences.en: Aaron swartz lost his life because he committed suicide.
sentences.zh: 亚伦·斯沃茨因自杀身亡。

sentences.en: Something he had tried multiple times before.
sentences.zh: 他之前已经尝试过很多次了。

sentences.en: If he really only committed suicide because of the legal jeopardy he was in, wouldn't it have made more sense to commit suicide after you're found guilty?
sentences.zh: 如果他真的只是因为陷入法律危险而自杀，那么在被判有罪后自杀不是更有意义吗？

blocks.html_en:
I'm sure it was. Let's also keep in mind that he was offered a 6 month plea bargain.

Yes, it sucks to accept a plea on something that you think was not illegal. But if we are going to argue that he killed himself because of the charges, we need to admit that he killed himself so he wouldn't have to spend 6 months in a minimum security prison. Heck, it might have even been house arrest.

sentences.en: I'm sure it was.
sentences.zh: 我确信是的。

sentences.en: Let's also keep in mind that he was offered a 6 month plea bargain.Yes, it sucks to accept a plea on something that you think was not illegal.
sentences.zh: 我们还要记住，他获得了 6 个月的认罪协议。是的，接受对你认为不违法的事情的认罪是很糟糕的。

sentences.en: But if we are going to argue that he killed himself because of the charges, we need to admit that he killed himself so he wouldn't have to spend 6 months in a minimum security prison.
sentences.zh: 但如果我们要争辩说他是因为指控而自杀，我们就需要承认他是自杀，这样他就不必在最低安全级别的监狱里度过 6 个月的时间。

sentences.en: Heck, it might have even been house arrest.
sentences.zh: 哎呀，甚至可能被软禁。

blocks.html_en:
Now view this in contrast with what happened to Aaron Swartz

> According to state and federal authorities, Swartz used JSTOR, a digital repository,[79] to download a large number[note 2] of academic journal articles through MIT's computer network over the course of a few weeks

> ...federal prosecutors filed a superseding indictment adding nine more felony counts, increasing Swartz's maximum criminal exposure to 50 years of imprisonment

> ...On the evening of January 11, 2013, Swartz's girlfriend, Stinebrickner-Kauffman, found him dead in his Brooklyn apartment.[80][116][117] A spokesperson for New York's Medical Examiner reported that he had hanged himself

sentences.en: Now view this in contrast with what happened to Aaron Swartz> According to state and federal authorities, Swartz used JSTOR, a digital repository,[79] to download a large number[note 2] of academic journal articles through MIT's computer network over the course of a few weeks> ...federal prosecutors filed a superseding indictment adding nine more felony counts, increasing Swartz's maximum criminal exposure to 50 years of imprisonment> ...On the evening of January 11, 2013, Swartz's girlfriend, Stinebrickner-Kauffman, found him dead in his Brooklyn apartment.[80][116][117] A spokesperson for New York's Medical Examiner reported that he had hanged himself
sentences.zh: 现在将此与亚伦·斯沃茨 (Aaron Swartz) 身上发生的事情进行对比> 根据州和联邦当局的说法，斯沃茨使用数字存储库 JSTOR，[79] 在几周内通过麻省理工学院的计算机网络下载了大量[注 2] 学术期刊文章> ...联邦检察官提出了一份替代起诉书，增加了 9 项重罪罪名，将斯沃茨的最高刑事犯罪风险提高到 50 年监禁> ...1 月晚上2013年11月11日，斯沃茨的女友斯汀布里克纳-考夫曼(Stinebrickner-Kauffman)发现他死在布鲁克林的公寓里。[80][116][117]纽约法医发言人称他是上吊自杀的

blocks.html_en:
Settlement would mean it doesn't become legal precedence, right?

This outcome seems to be the best possible for Anthropic. Over 100B$ have been invested in AI so far, venture capital can afford to pay a few billions per big company as a South Park style "Sorry".

Or am I missing something?

sentences.en: Settlement would mean it doesn't become legal precedence, right?This outcome seems to be the best possible for Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 和解意味着它不会成为法律优先权，对吗？这个结果似乎对 Anthropic 来说是最好的结果。

sentences.zh: 到目前为止，人工智能领域的投资已超过 100 巴西利亚，风险投资可以为每个大公司支付数十亿美元，就像南方公园风格的“对不起”。还是我错过了什么？

blocks.html_en:
> Settlement would mean it doesn't become legal precedence, right?

The summary judgment ruling from 2025 in this case will still be legal precedence, the settlement will mean that there is no trial for damages.

However, since this is a district court ruling it is non-binding on other courts.

sentences.en: > Settlement would mean it doesn't become legal precedence, right?The summary judgment ruling from 2025 in this case will still be legal precedence, the settlement will mean that there is no trial for damages.However, since this is a district court ruling it is non-binding on other courts.
sentences.zh: > 和解意味着它不会成为法律优先例，对吧？从2025年开始，本案的简易判决裁决仍然具有法律优先例，和解意味着不会对损害赔偿进行审判。但是，由于这是地方法院的裁决，因此对其他法院不具有约束力。

blocks.html_en:
This is not even than a slap on the wrist. Publishers who negotiated this really fucked up writers.

According to US federal law, pirating a single copyrighted work and gaining commercial advantage of it (which Anthropic 100% did) represents five years in prison and a $250,000 fine. But it gets worse:

"Penalties for a copyright infringement conviction may increase if the defendant has previous similar convictions, made more than 10 copies of copyrighted works, committed copyright infringement during a period longer than 180 days, or infringed copyrighted material worth more than $2,500."

https://www.justia.com/entertainment-law/piracy-in-the-enter...

sentences.en: This is not even than a slap on the wrist.
sentences.zh: 这连巴掌都算不上。

sentences.en: Publishers who negotiated this really fucked up writers.According to US federal law, pirating a single copyrighted work and gaining commercial advantage of it (which Anthropic 100% did) represents five years in prison and a $250,000 fine.
sentences.zh: 进行谈判的出版商真是把作家搞砸了。根据美国联邦法律，盗版一部受版权保护的作品并从中获得商业优势（Anthropic 100% 就是这样做的）意味着五年监禁和 25 万美元罚款。

sentences.en: But it gets worse:"Penalties for a copyright infringement conviction may increase if the defendant has previous similar convictions, made more than 10 copies of copyrighted works, committed copyright infringement during a period longer than 180 days, or infringed copyrighted material worth more than $2,500."https://www.justia.com/entertainment-law/piracy-in-the-enter...
sentences.zh: 但情况变得更糟：“如果被告以前有类似的定罪，制作超过 10 份受版权保护的作品副本，在超过 180 天的时间内侵犯版权，或侵犯价值超过 2,500 美元的受版权保护的材料，则对版权侵权定罪的处罚可能会增加。”https://www.justia.com/entertainment-law/piracy-in-the-enter...

blocks.html_en:
I sincerely don't understand what the point of these laws are, when the cost of flagrant violations is no more than a slap on the wrist -- these really meager sums that serve as nothing more than something to point at and say "Look, we did something!"

Cover-your-ass strategy, and nothing more. Who, besides the ones at fault, are ever happy with these mean-nothing fines?

The justice system really needs an overhaul with how it tackles "justice" between the wealthy, the connected, the corporations, and the rest. Though I am unsure what that would look like. Minimum net wealth per category of infraction across the board?

Edit: grammar

sentences.en: I sincerely don't understand what the point of these laws are, when the cost of flagrant violations is no more than a slap on the wrist -- these really meager sums that serve as nothing more than something to point at and say "Look, we did something!"Cover-your-ass strategy, and nothing more.
sentences.zh: 我真诚地不明白这些法律的意义是什么，当公然违法的成本不过是小事一桩——这些微不足道的金额只不过是用来指出并说“看，我们做了一些事情！”掩护你的屁股策略，仅此而已。

sentences.en: Who, besides the ones at fault, are ever happy with these mean-nothing fines?The justice system really needs an overhaul with how it tackles "justice" between the wealthy, the connected, the corporations, and the rest.
sentences.zh: 除了那些有过错的人之外，还有谁会对这些毫无意义的罚款感到高兴呢？司法系统确实需要彻底改革，如何处理富人、有联系的人、公司和其他人之间的“正义”。

sentences.en: Though I am unsure what that would look like.
sentences.zh: 虽然我不确定那会是什么样子。

sentences.en: Minimum net wealth per category of infraction across the board?Edit: grammar
sentences.zh: 全面每类违规行为的最低净财富？编辑：语法

blocks.html_en:
This is a settlement that the authors and Anthropic agreed upon.

They agreed on the amount last year. The judge approved it now.

The lawsuit was for the way the books were acquired. They already ruled that it's not infringement to use the books.

The award was $3,000 per book, which is about 100X higher than it would have cost to buy the books.

It's never going to appease the people who demand companies be sued into collapse, but given that both parties came to an agreement and the damages are 100X higher than what a book costs, it looks reasonable to me.

sentences.en: This is a settlement that the authors and Anthropic agreed upon.They agreed on the amount last year.
sentences.zh: 这是作者和 Anthropic 达成的和解协议。他们去年就金额达成了一致。

sentences.en: The judge approved it now.The lawsuit was for the way the books were acquired.
sentences.zh: 现在法官批准了。这起诉讼是针对书籍的获取方式。

sentences.en: They already ruled that it's not infringement to use the books.The award was $3,000 per book, which is about 100X higher than it would have cost to buy the books.It's never going to appease the people who demand companies be sued into collapse, but given that both parties came to an agreement and the damages are 100X higher than what a book costs, it looks reasonable to me.
sentences.zh: 他们已经裁定使用这些书不构成侵权。赔偿金为每本书 3,000 美元，这比购买这些书的成本高出大约 100 倍。它永远不会安抚那些要求公司被起诉到倒闭的人，但考虑到双方达成了协议，而且损害赔偿比一本书的成本高出 100 倍，这在我看来是合理的。

blocks.html_en:
Of course it is. If I write a movie review and sell it to a magazine or whatever, it's derived from the movie, and it's fair use, and I don't need to ask the movie owner for permission first, or give them a cut of my sales. Even if I use some reasonable number of screenshots and video clips, as long as the resulting work is "transformative" i.e. actually a new work, a movie review instead of a copy of the movie.

Do you want this to work any other way? I constantly see people in the AI debate working themselves into wildly copyright maximalist positions. I actually don't think that we should give every author veto power over a book review!

sentences.en: Of course it is.

sentences.zh: 我经常看到人工智能辩论中的人们将自己置于极端版权最大化的立场。

blocks.html_en:
>I constantly see people in the AI debate working themselves into wildly copyright maximalist positions

I really dont get this. I know its that conflation fallacy or whatever, but I was under the impression we had sort of gotten over copyright maximalism as a society after Napster etc.

Whats worse is that, meaningful reform in this space has basically been waiting on a multi billion dollar corporation to come along and push it forward. So now that we have an opportunity to expand and globalise fair use, the sudden and quite angry opposition weirds me out to no end.

sentences.en: >I constantly see people in the AI debate working themselves into wildly copyright maximalist positionsI really dont get this.
sentences.zh: >我经常看到人工智能辩论中的人们陷入版权极端主义的立场，我真的不明白这一点。

sentences.zh: 我知道这是合并谬误或其他什么，但我的印象是，在 Napster 等之后，我们作为一个社会已经克服了版权最大化主义。更糟糕的是，这个领域的有意义的改革基本上一直在等待一家价值数十亿美元的公司出现并推动它向前发展。

sentences.en: So now that we have an opportunity to expand and globalise fair use, the sudden and quite angry opposition weirds me out to no end.
sentences.zh: 因此，现在我们有机会扩大和全球化合理使用，突然而相当愤怒的反对让我感到很奇怪。

blocks.html_en:
There's two issues with copyright.

1. author owns the right to distribute copies of the work

2. this right goes on for faaaaaar too long.

I don't have an issue with 1. You had a good idea, you implemented it, you deserve something for it. Given some people got sued into oblivion with ridiculous dollar value outcomes on a per unit basis - why doesn't this apply here? Sure 1.5 billion is a lot. But the number of infringments is insane and the company is approaching a trillion in valuation. You could make it ten times that number.

I do have an issue with 2. Sure, you had a good idea, you implemented it, you deserve something for it. But after 20 years, you should be able to come up with another idea or just work like the rest of us. Going for 50, 70, 90+ years with the rewards going to estate heirs? Fuck that.

So yeah, I am both against copyright AND surprised at the slap on the wrist for what happened here.

sentences.zh: 这个权利持续了太久了。我对 1 没有意见。

sentences.zh: 你有一个好主意，你实施了它，你应该得到一些东西。

sentences.zh: 鉴于有些人因每单位的可笑的美元价值结果而被起诉而被遗忘 - 为什么这在这里不适用？

sentences.zh: 15亿当然是很多了。

sentences.en: But the number of infringments is insane and the company is approaching a trillion in valuation.
sentences.zh: 但侵权数量惊人，公司估值也接近万亿。

sentences.en: You could make it ten times that number.I do have an issue with 2.
sentences.zh: 你可以把它变成这个数字的十倍。我对 2 确实有意见。

sentences.en: Going for 50, 70, 90+ years with the rewards going to estate heirs?
sentences.zh: 50年、70年、90年以上，遗产继承人都会得到回报吗？

sentences.en: Fuck that.So yeah, I am both against copyright AND surprised at the slap on the wrist for what happened here.
sentences.zh: 去他妈的。所以，是的，我既反对版权，又对这里发生的事情受到的惩罚感到惊讶。

blocks.html_en:
>Given some people got sued into oblivion with ridiculous dollar value outcomes on a per unit basis - why doesn't this apply here?

I mean, it feels to me like one or both of:

1. The class action lawyers werent 100% certain they could win in court. 2. The class action lawyers smelled an easy payday.

They get ~100 million out of this.

I also think that the 1500 bucks going to most of these authors is going to be more than they ever saw in royalties. I read somewhere that 500 - 1500 bucks is roughly what a self pub book makes in its lifetime. Why push the envelope? Anthropic hasnt done anything that deserves to pay for the entire lifetime royalties of most books. Their legal alternative is to cut the spine off and scan the book in. In which case the author and publisher will be splitting 20 bucks instead, assuming Anthropic isnt buying used.

This seems like a donation tbh.

>slap on the wrist for what happened here.

Its not a punishment at all because this is a civil case that has been settled out of court.

blocks.html_en: IANAL but as an IP creator I have not heard of "derivative products" in the copyright context. There are "derivative works", which are covered by the same copyright as the original. For example, a translation to another language is a derivative work, a novelisation of a movie, a screen adaptation of a book etc. If some author could have proven that any Anthromic model is a derivative work of theirs then they had the copyright on that model and made mad bucks licensing it back to Anthropic.

sentences.en: IANAL but as an IP creator I have not heard of "derivative products" in the copyright context.
sentences.zh: IANAL，但作为知识产权创造者，我还没有听说过版权方面的“衍生产品”。

sentences.en: There are "derivative works", which are covered by the same copyright as the original.
sentences.zh: 还有“衍生作品”，它们与原作具有相同的版权。

sentences.en: For example, a translation to another language is a derivative work, a novelisation of a movie, a screen adaptation of a book etc. If some author could have proven that any Anthromic model is a derivative work of theirs then they had the copyright on that model and made mad bucks licensing it back to Anthropic.
sentences.zh: 例如，翻译成另一种语言就是衍生作品、电影小说、书籍改编作品等。如果某个作者能够证明任何 Anthromic 模型都是他们的衍生作品，那么他们就拥有该模型的版权，并赚了很多钱，将其授权给 Anthropic。

blocks.html_en:
To sign up for what? The experience of approximately every author on the planet is that they found out that Anthropic did something bad at the same time they were "opted into" the class. The only thing they could do is opt out and litigate on their own against a company with a valuation approaching $1T.

This is a sweet deal for lawyers and for publishers, and nothing else.

Thats more than it costs to just shred the spine and scan the book in. Which is probably 15 - 20 bucks a piece.

They will be shredding the book not paying the fine.

blocks.html_en: Civil justice is primarily about restoring damages, not about punishing wrongdoing (although common law in US it is more punitive than civil law in european countries). Therefore compensations are based on damages, not on profit from wrongdoings.

blocks.html_en:
For the downvotes, my response is to read: https://authorsguild.org/advocacy/artificial-intelligence/wh...

> If there is a current publisher(s) (which still possesses an exclusive license), the author(s) will split the $3000 with the publisher. Any co-authors will share the author portion and, if there are multiple publishers (e.g., different publishers have exclusive rights to different formats), they will share the publisher portion. Assume that the co-authors and co-publishers will share the portion equally unless their contracts provide otherwise. The standard default split between publishers and authors of noneducational texts is 50/50, as described below. Authors who are the sole rightsholder in a work—such as self-published authors and authors whose rights have reverted or where the contracts have otherwise terminated—will receive the full award amount.

It is split between the publisher and the author, also publishers will have a large catalog of books they will submit, an author typically will only have a few -- the payout will be going to the lawyers and mostly to publishers.

sentences.zh: 假设共同作者和共同出版商将平均分享该部分，除非他们的合同另有规定。

sentences.en: The standard default split between publishers and authors of noneducational texts is 50/50, as described below.
sentences.zh: 非教育文本的出版商和作者之间的标准默认分配是 50/50，如下所述。

sentences.en: Authors who are the sole rightsholder in a work—such as self-published authors and authors whose rights have reverted or where the contracts have otherwise terminated—will receive the full award amount.It is split between the publisher and the author, also publishers will have a large catalog of books they will submit, an author typically will only have a few -- the payout will be going to the lawyers and mostly to publishers.
sentences.zh: 作为作品的唯一权利持有人的作者（例如自行出版的作者和权利已恢复或合同已终止的作者）将获得全额奖金。奖金由出版商和作者分配，出版商将提交大量图书，而作者通常只有少数图书 - 奖金将支付给律师，大部分支付给出版商。

blocks.html_en:
>It is split between the publisher and the author, also publishers will have a large catalog of books they will submit, an author typically will only have a few -- the payout will be going to the lawyers and mostly to publishers.

This is innumerate. If it's split 50% between authors and publishers, then it won't be "mostly to publishers". Mathematically it will be equal between "authors" and "publishers", and because lawyers are taking their cut, neither would be able to get "most" of it. Yes, the average publisher will get a bigger paycheck, but that's because there's less of them, not because "most going to publishers".

sentences.en: >It is split between the publisher and the author, also publishers will have a large catalog of books they will submit, an author typically will only have a few -- the payout will be going to the lawyers and mostly to publishers.This is innumerate.
sentences.zh: >它是由出版商和作者分开的，而且出版商将提交大量的书籍，而作者通常只有少数——报酬将支付给律师，大部分支付给出版商。这是无数的。

sentences.en: If it's split 50% between authors and publishers, then it won't be "mostly to publishers".
sentences.zh: 如果作者和出版商各占 50%，那么就不会“主要分配给出版商”。

sentences.en: Mathematically it will be equal between "authors" and "publishers", and because lawyers are taking their cut, neither would be able to get "most" of it.
sentences.zh: 从数学上讲，“作者”和“出版商”之间的收入是平等的，而且由于律师正在分一杯羹，所以双方都无法获得“大部分”。

sentences.en: Yes, the average publisher will get a bigger paycheck, but that's because there's less of them, not because "most going to publishers".
sentences.zh: 是的，普通出版商会得到更高的薪水，但那是因为他们的数量较少，而不是因为“大多数都流向了出版商”。

blocks.html_en:
> That means that rightsholders can expect at least $3,000 per title (less costs and fees), which will be shared among the rightsholders for that title (if there is more than one rightsholder)

> if there is more than one rightsholder

Again, a publisher will have a whole catalog of books / titles, a non-negligible portion of that the publisher will own the copyright to (no one to split it with). There's all kinds of books outside of novels, there's media tie-ins, IP franchise books (ie Star Wars), childrens books, textbooks / reference materials, etc etc etc. Yes, with novels the author tends to own the copyright, but you're forgetting all of the other kinds of books out there.

sentences.en: > That means that rightsholders can expect at least $3,000 per title (less costs and fees), which will be shared among the rightsholders for that title (if there is more than one rightsholder)> if there is more than one rightsholderAgain, a publisher will have a whole catalog of books / titles, a non-negligible portion of that the publisher will own the copyright to (no one to split it with).
sentences.zh: > 这意味着权利持有者可以期望每本书至少获得 3,000 美元（减去成本和费用），该费用将在该书的权利持有者之间共享（如果有多个权利持有者）> 如果有多个权利持有者，出版商将拥有一整套书籍/书名，出版商将拥有其中不可忽视的部分版权（没有人可以分割它）。

sentences.en: There's all kinds of books outside of novels, there's media tie-ins, IP franchise books (ie Star Wars), childrens books, textbooks / reference materials, etc etc etc. Yes, with novels the author tends to own the copyright, but you're forgetting all of the other kinds of books out there.
sentences.zh: 除了小说之外，还有各种各样的书籍，还有媒体搭配、IP特许经营书籍（即星球大战）、儿童读物、教科书/参考资料等等。是的，小说的作者往往拥有版权，但你忘记了所有其他类型的书籍。

Again, a publisher will have a whole catalog of books / titles, a non-negligible portion of that the publisher will own the copyright to (no one to split it with). There's all kinds of books outside of novels, there's media tie-ins, IP franchise books (ie Star Wars), childrens books, textbooks / reference materials, etc etc etc. Yes, with novels the author tends to own the copyright, but you're forgetting all of the other kinds of books out there.

blocks.html_en:
> I sincerely don't understand what the point of these laws are, when the cost of flagrant violations is no more than a slap on the wrist -- these really meager sums that serve as nothing more than something to point at and say "Look, we did something!"

To create a moat around wealth generation. After all, that is the main purpose of all legal systems---to keep the wealthy wealthy and the poor poor. In this case, the settlement is chump change for Anthropic, but ensures that no upstart will be able to compete with them since they will get reamed on copyright charges. It's no different from Google Image search. They can make a product out of republishing others' images. You cannot do it.

sentences.en: > I sincerely don't understand what the point of these laws are, when the cost of flagrant violations is no more than a slap on the wrist -- these really meager sums that serve as nothing more than something to point at and say "Look, we did something!"To create a moat around wealth generation.

blocks.html_en:
To keep users paying for content while companies do whatever they want - and if that's not the reason that's certainly an effect.

> The justice system really needs an overhaul with how it tackles "justice" between the wealthy, the connected, the corporations, and the rest. Though I am unsure what that would look like. Minimum net wealth per category of infraction across the board?

% of annual turnover seems like decent strategy. Caps the amount company can sue mere mortal for copyright infringement while at billion dollar company scale can wipe quite a bit

But main problem is enforcement and lobbying, not the size of the fine

blocks.html_en:
IMO this ruling will be the inflection point that kills either the book publishing industry or the US big model frontier lab industry.

It's trendy to say it'll be the later, but I see a credible case for the former.

I see no reason to pay for a textbook in 2026, while I'm happy to pay an expensive monthly subscription for a coding agent.

(And before someone accuses me of being anti education or anything, bona fide scientist with a PhD here and I have written book chapters for a couple of popular textbooks).

sentences.en: IMO this ruling will be the inflection point that kills either the book publishing industry or the US big model frontier lab industry.It's trendy to say it'll be the later, but I see a credible case for the former.I see no reason to pay for a textbook in 2026, while I'm happy to pay an expensive monthly subscription for a coding agent.(And before someone accuses me of being anti education or anything, bona fide scientist with a PhD here and I have written book chapters for a couple of popular textbooks).
sentences.zh: 在我看来，这一裁决将成为扼杀图书出版业或美国大型模型前沿实验室行业的拐点。流行的说法是后者，但我看到了前者的可信案例。我认为没有理由在 2026 年支付教科书费用，而我很乐意为编码代理支付昂贵的每月订阅费用。（在有人指责我反教育或其他什么之前，这里是真正拥有博士学位的科学家，我已经写了书籍章节）一些流行的教科书）。

blocks.html_en:
I'm glad to see Anthropic's nose bloodied but I'm still very worried about the ruling in this case as I can already see the wheels turning as a way to limit fair use.

For context, the ruling is basically, "AI training is fair use but building a library of pirated books to train on is not". This is obviously because Judge Alsup does not want to put AI under a de-facto ban, but he wants AI companies to have to care about copyright... which in my opinion is self-contradictory, but let's go along with the (paraconsistent) logic.

If we insist that every prior act up to a fair use must be lawful, then this means that fair use is not a right, but a privilege that is purchased alongside the work itself. This opens the door to Oracle-level shenanigans: so long as every legal avenue to watch a work is encumbered by, say, a DeWitt clause[0], you cannot legally review the work. There are actually copyright cases hinging on this: Triller Fight Club sued H3H3 for reviewing a pirated stream of a Logan Paul fight that lasted 40 seconds and lost, for obvious reasons. This case smells like an accidental overturning of this.

Would I rather live in a world where robots[1] aren't allowed to read copyrighted books, or a world where copyright owners have veto rights over any and all critical commentary of their work? I would happily choose the former every time.

[0] A contractual clause that prohibits the recipient of a work from reviewing it without written permission of the owner.

[1] Mind uploads inclusive

sentences.en: I'm glad to see Anthropic's nose bloodied but I'm still very worried about the ruling in this case as I can already see the wheels turning as a way to limit fair use.For context, the ruling is basically, "AI training is fair use but building a library of pirated books to train on is not".
sentences.zh: 我很高兴看到 Anthropic 的鼻子流血，但我仍然非常担心本案的裁决，因为我已经可以看到车轮转动作为限制合理使用的一种方式。就上下文而言，裁决基本上是，“人工智能训练是合理使用，但建立一个盗版书籍库来训练不是”。

sentences.en: This is obviously because Judge Alsup does not want to put AI under a de-facto ban, but he wants AI companies to have to care about copyright...
sentences.zh: 这显然是因为阿尔苏普法官不想将人工智能置于事实上的禁令之下，但他希望人工智能公司必须关心版权……

sentences.en: which in my opinion is self-contradictory, but let's go along with the (paraconsistent) logic.If we insist that every prior act up to a fair use must be lawful, then this means that fair use is not a right, but a privilege that is purchased alongside the work itself.
sentences.zh: 在我看来，这是自相矛盾的，但让我们遵循（次一致）逻辑。如果我们坚持认为合理使用之前的每一项行为都必须合法，那么这意味着合理使用不是一种权利，而是一种与作品本身一起购买的特权。

sentences.en: This opens the door to Oracle-level shenanigans: so long as every legal avenue to watch a work is encumbered by, say, a DeWitt clause[0], you cannot legally review the work.
sentences.zh: 这为神谕级的恶作剧打开了大门：只要观看作品的每条合法途径都受到德威特条款[0]等的阻碍，你就无法合法地审查该作品。

sentences.en: There are actually copyright cases hinging on this: Triller Fight Club sued H3H3 for reviewing a pirated stream of a Logan Paul fight that lasted 40 seconds and lost, for obvious reasons.
sentences.zh: 实际上，版权案件与此相关：Triller Fight Club 起诉 H3H3，因为 H3H3 审核了一段持续 40 秒的 Logan Paul 拳击比赛的盗版直播，但最终败诉，原因显而易见。

sentences.en: This case smells like an accidental overturning of this.Would I rather live in a world where robots[1] aren't allowed to read copyrighted books, or a world where copyright owners have veto rights over any and all critical commentary of their work?

blocks.html_en:
I'm glad to see Anthropic's nose bloodied but I'm still very worried about the ruling in this case as I can already see the wheels turning as a way to limit fair use.

For context, the ruling is basically, "AI training is fair use but building a library of pirated books to train on is not". This is obviously because Judge Alsup does not want to put AI under a de-facto ban, but he wants AI companies to have to care about copyright... which in my opinion is self-contradictory, but let's go along with the (paraconsistent) logic.

If we insist that every prior act up to a fair use must be lawful, then this means that fair use is not a right, but a privilege that is purchased alongside the work itself. This opens the door to Oracle-level shenanigans: so long as every legal avenue to watch a work is encumbered by, say, a DeWitt clause[0], you cannot legally review the work. There are actually copyright cases hinging on this: Triller Fight Club sued H3H3 for reviewing a pirated stream of a Logan Paul fight that lasted 40 seconds and lost, for obvious reasons. This case smells like an accidental overturning of this.

Would I rather live in a world where robots[1] aren't allowed to read copyrighted books, or a world where copyright owners have veto rights over any and all critical commentary of their work? I would happily choose the former every time.

[0] A contractual clause that prohibits the recipient of a work from reviewing it without written permission of the owner.

[1] Mind uploads inclusive

blocks.html_en:
So it goes like this: I first you take, then money you make and eventually you repay. This is a very smart loan from society indeed. And seems to be the new normal…

Even though Anthropic is my daily driver I’m done respecting any sort of copyright. I’m okay paying for subscription for a service delivery but never ever again will I believe in copyright or any other utterly non-enforceable similar concept.

sentences.en: This is a very smart loan from society indeed.
sentences.zh: 这确实是一笔非常明智的社会贷款。

sentences.zh: 我可以接受付费订阅服务，但我再也不会相信版权或任何其他完全不可执行的类似概念。

blocks.html_en:
There should be no such thing as "too big to fail" in a free, competitive market. Companies must be allowed to fail.

If failure means catastrophe for the nation, it shouldn't have been a private, for-profit project in the first place and instead be a public project.

sentences.en: There should be no such thing as "too big to fail" in a free, competitive market.
sentences.zh: 在自由、竞争的市场中不应该存在“太大而不能倒”的情况。

sentences.en: Companies must be allowed to fail.If failure means catastrophe for the nation, it shouldn't have been a private, for-profit project in the first place and instead be a public project.

blocks.html_en:
Honestly maybe (and just maybe) waiving copyrights on all the written content that ever existed to create training data would be a good thing to do, laws are made up so we can decide it’s a good trade off as a society. But:

1. I feel like this should be discussed globally, there should be a public debate, a vote, and guardrails

2. It should not be in the hands of private companies, it should either be done by the government and made available to the public ; or if it’s done by private companies they should be mandated to give the training data to the government so it’s available to the public.

My point is we can decide to say it’s ok because LLMs are too important strategically. But if we do so it should benefit the public, not 5 mega corporations, training data should be considered as public infrastructure, like roads, rails, or the electricity grid. Societies are failing and this is just one more nail in the coffin.

sentences.en: Honestly maybe (and just maybe) waiving copyrights on all the written content that ever existed to create training data would be a good thing to do, laws are made up so we can decide it’s a good trade off as a society.
sentences.zh: 老实说，也许（只是也许）放弃所有用于创建训练数据的书面内容的版权将是一件好事，法律是制定出来的，所以我们可以决定作为一个社会这是一个很好的权衡。

sentences.en: But:1.
sentences.zh: 但是：1。

sentences.en: I feel like this should be discussed globally, there should be a public debate, a vote, and guardrails2.
sentences.zh: 我觉得这应该在全球范围内进行讨论，应该进行公开辩论、投票和护栏2。

sentences.en: It should not be in the hands of private companies, it should either be done by the government and made available to the public ; or if it’s done by private companies they should be mandated to give the training data to the government so it’s available to the public.My point is we can decide to say it’s ok because LLMs are too important strategically.

sentences.en: But if we do so it should benefit the public, not 5 mega corporations, training data should be considered as public infrastructure, like roads, rails, or the electricity grid.

sentences.en: Societies are failing and this is just one more nail in the coffin.
sentences.zh: 社会正在走向失败，这只是棺材上的又一颗钉子。

blocks.html_en:
Honestly maybe (and just maybe) waiving copyrights on all the written content that ever existed to create training data would be a good thing to do, laws are made up so we can decide it’s a good trade off as a society. But:

1. I feel like this should be discussed globally, there should be a public debate, a vote, and guardrails

2. It should not be in the hands of private companies, it should either be done by the government and made available to the public ; or if it’s done by private companies they should be mandated to give the training data to the government so it’s available to the public.

My point is we can decide to say it’s ok because LLMs are too important strategically. But if we do so it should benefit the public, not 5 mega corporations, training data should be considered as public infrastructure, like roads, rails, or the electricity grid. Societies are failing and this is just one more nail in the coffin.

blocks.html_en:
One thing I always wondered ...

There used to be libgen. Then it went down. It went semi-back up but ... it is still kind of down.

Those issues kind of coincided with the big greedy mega-corporations leeching off data en masse; Anthropic was not the only one, Facebook is another example here. I always wondered whether the decline in quality, fewer liberated books published, coincided with what the big corporations were doing. Would be great to be able to see any underlying strategy here. Imagine Anthropic, just as a scenario, leeching off of everyone else, and then also sending in their lawyers to try to close down what they leeched off here. I mean the rise of bots kind of coincides with the rise of AI. So why not them also trying to make it harder for the rest of the world to access liberated books.

sentences.zh: 我一直想知道的一件事......曾经有libgen。

sentences.zh: 然后它就下降了。

sentences.en: It went semi-back up but ...
sentences.zh: 它半恢复了，但是......

sentences.zh: 这些问题与贪婪的大公司大规模窃取数据同时发生； Anthropic 并不是唯一的例子，Facebook 是另一个例子。

sentences.en: I always wondered whether the decline in quality, fewer liberated books published, coincided with what the big corporations were doing.
sentences.zh: 我一直想知道质量的下降、出版的解放书籍的减少是否与大公司的做法相一致。

sentences.en: Would be great to be able to see any underlying strategy here.
sentences.zh: 能够在这里看到任何基本策略真是太好了。

sentences.zh: 我的意思是机器人的兴起与人工智能的兴起同时发生。

sentences.en: So why not them also trying to make it harder for the rest of the world to access liberated books.
sentences.zh: 那么为什么他们不尝试让世界其他地方更难获得解放的书籍呢？

blocks.html_en:
That depends on the answer to a question that hasn't been answered yet.

Is what an AI does similar to a human reading a book, and adding it to their knowledge? Or is it similar to a human plagiarizing a book? If it's the second, for at least some books, no, the damages are not reasonable. They are far too small.

blocks.html_en:
> That depends on the answer to a question that hasn't been answered yet.

It has been answered in a sense, because the courts (so far) have ruled that training is Fair Use. Whether this is similar to a human learning from a book was not quite the question being answered, but AFAICT there is no other relevant doctrine under Copyright law to address it, largely because the question didn't even exist until LLMs came along.

Also, these are not damages, it's a settlement i.e. a negotiated agreement between both parties.

Relevant sub-thread here: https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48997766

sentences.zh: > 这取决于尚未回答的问题的答案。从某种意义上说已经得到了回答，因为法院（到目前为止）已裁定培训属于合理使用。

sentences.zh: 这是否类似于人类从书本中学习并不是要回答的问题，但AFAICT在版权法下没有其他相关的学说来解决这个问题，很大程度上是因为这个问题在LLM出现之前根本不存在。而且，这些不是损害赔偿，而是双方之间协商达成的协议。相关子线程在这里：https://news.ycombinator.com/item?id=48997766

blocks.html_en: It has been answered in a sense, because the courts (so far) have ruled that training is Fair Use. Whether this is similar to a human learning from a book was not quite the question being answered, but AFAICT there is no other relevant doctrine under Copyright law to address it, largely because the question didn't even exist until LLMs came along.

blocks.html_en:
Good question. Can you ask an LLM to repeat the entire contents of a novel, word-for-word, and read that instead of the original book? I haven't tried it, but I would guess it would not be able to do this.

Can you ask it questions about the book and expect it to get them right? Yeah, probably. Same as if I read the book and you asked me questions about it. The LLM would probably answer those questions better than I could, and about every single book in its training data, but still same-same.

I don't think this is plaguarism.

blocks.html_en:
As was pointed out, the settlement is for piracy, not training. They had already ruled that Anthropic's use of copyrighted material for training fell within fair use.

As such, if you pirated a book and had to pay $3000 for that one instance, I don't think you'd like it if I said you should have paid $30K or $300K instead. If anything, this is analogous to the ridiculous fines people had to pay when pirating music.

(Not that I'm complaining...)

sentences.en: As was pointed out, the settlement is for piracy, not training.
sentences.zh: 正如所指出的，和解协议是针对盗版行为，而不是培训。

sentences.zh: 他们已经裁定 Anthropic 使用受版权保护的材料进行培训属于合理使用范围。因此，如果您盗版了一本书并且必须为此支付 3000 美元，如果我说您应该支付 3 万美元或 30 万美元，我认为您不会喜欢它。

sentences.zh: 如果有的话，这类似于人们在盗版音乐时必须支付的荒谬罚款。（并不是我在抱怨......）

blocks.html_en:
> As such, if you pirated a book and had to pay $3000 for that one instance, I don't think you'd like it if I said you should have paid $30K or $300K instead.

If you pirated a book for personal use the amount of liability wouldn't match a company whose profit could be attributed to pirating the same book. In US copyright law, a copyright infringer could be liable for "any profits of the infringer that are attributable to the infringement" [1] (if the copyright owner elects to recover actual damages and profits instead of statutory damages).

[1] 17 U.S.C. § 504(b), https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/17/504

sentences.en: > As such, if you pirated a book and had to pay $3000 for that one instance, I don't think you'd like it if I said you should have paid $30K or $300K instead.If you pirated a book for personal use the amount of liability wouldn't match a company whose profit could be attributed to pirating the same book.
sentences.zh: > 因此，如果您盗版一本书并且必须为此支付 3000 美元，如果我说您应该支付 3 万美元或 30 万美元，我认为您不会喜欢。如果您盗版一本书供个人使用，则责任金额与一家因盗版同一本书而获利的公司不相称。

sentences.en: In US copyright law, a copyright infringer could be liable for "any profits of the infringer that are attributable to the infringement" [1] (if the copyright owner elects to recover actual damages and profits instead of statutory damages).[1] 17 U.S.C.
sentences.zh: 在美国版权法中，版权侵权者可能要对“侵权者因侵权而获得的任何利润”承担责任[1]（如果版权所有者选择追回实际损失和利润，而不是法定损失）。[1] 17 美国法典

sentences.en: § 504(b), https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/17/504
sentences.zh: § 504(b)，https://www.law.cornell.edu/uscode/text/17/504

blocks.html_en:
I would imagine that for over 99% of the books covered in this lawsuit, they're earning less than $3000 per book.

Put another way, their revenues wouldn't drop much if they simply hadn't trained on those 99%.

blocks.html_en:
IANAL, but the parent comment quotes "any profits of the infringer that are attributable to the infringement", which I take to mean it's the profit Anthropic stands to make based on its use of the pirated content that's recoverable.

Given the entire global economy is currently bullish on the potential profitability of AI, I dare say they got off incredibly lightly settling for just $3k per book.

sentences.en: IANAL, but the parent comment quotes "any profits of the infringer that are attributable to the infringement", which I take to mean it's the profit Anthropic stands to make based on its use of the pirated content that's recoverable.Given the entire global economy is currently bullish on the potential profitability of AI, I dare say they got off incredibly lightly settling for just $3k per book.
sentences.zh: IANAL，但母公司的评论引用了“侵权者因侵权而获得的任何利润”，我认为这意味着 Anthropic 基于其使用可收回的盗版内容而获得的利润。鉴于目前整个全球经济都看好人工智能的潜在盈利能力，我敢说他们轻松地以每本书 3000 美元的价格达成了协议。

blocks.html_en:
None of this matters, this is the judge approving a voluntary settlement reached between the parties last year.

If you think it should be different then you have to make a cogent argument why the public should get to interfere with a settlement the two sides mutually agree on.

blocks.html_en:
No, because you cannot prove that any individual book actually contributed meaningfully to Anthropic’s profit.

Exclude one book from the training dataset.

Did you make a worse model?

We actually know the answer to this, and it is: absolutely not.

The reality is this: your intellectual output is almost always only valuable to any company in existence in aggregate, never in isolation.

blocks.html_en:
I work as an author. I believe this is total bullshit, from beginning to end - the ruling, the settlement, and the suit itself.

In the UK, we have a thing called the Public Lending Right [1]. This pays authors a fixed sum each time their book is taken out of a library, up to a capped amount.

The cap isn't very high - about $7k - so it is both an OK bit of income for authors who might be making very little money elsewhere, and also doesn't end up all going to authors who are already bestsellers. It's a decent legal system for helping libraries hold niche titles as well as the popular ones. This is, after all, the purpose of a library.

To establish my bias here: My debut novel came out after the period this specific suit concerns. I also uploaded it to LibGen myself.

I strongly believe that books should be available to read, free of charge, to all people. I benefited enormously from libraries and piracy growing up. I think they serve an important educational purpose that does not end when a person leaves school, and I do not think wealth or disposable income is a fair way to decide the breadth of a person's education.

I also have no problem with people making new "language things" using my work. I love sample-based music (like dance music, hip hop, etc) and it'd be hypocritical for me to take issue with anyone doing analogous things using books. Maximising sales is not the end-goal of making art, for me personally. Other artists feel otherwise. They consider training on pirated books stealing. That's OK - it's not for me to tell them what to believe.

The problem for me is that these corporations - undoubtedly still pretraining on pirated material - are, essentially, leeching. By not releasing the model as open-weight, freely available, they are not acting in the same spirit of the system they took advantage of. It's the Spotify model: pirate first, pay a nominal amount that does not meaningfully harm profit later. Now the dust has settled there, we can see the harm it has done to music culture.

A single settlement which does not establish precedent does not solve anything. A tokenistic $3k allows anti-AI authors to wave a cheque in the air and declare a victory. It pays the rent for a month or two. It does nothing for the months after that, when the corporation is still profiting. It does nothing to establish precedent for future artists, who also have to pay rent.

It would be (non-trivial, but) relatively simple to integrate - for example - download figures from Anna's Archive into the PLR. I'd happily dilute my PLR payment appropriately, because I think libraries are important.

You can't stop people pirating digitally replicable things. Digital ownership is not a concept that has held, or will hold.

There are only 23,000 authors in the UK who claim the cash from the PLR. To pay all those authors the national living wage in the UK (£26k) from the PLR, you would need to raise £546 million. That is around 1/34 of Anthropic's reported annual revenue.

I'm of course not arguing Anthropic should be solely responsible. But it's very frustrating that all the pieces of the puzzle for actually paying artists in a sustainable and ongoing way now exist, and one of the major obstacles to this - and the idea of a genuinely free, legal, international library, which creates more authors, writing better books, full-time - are legacy rights holders who remain attached to a completely dysfunctional and outdated concept of ownership.

So - unless part of a sustained and reasonable campaign, which understands the futility of (and damage to the medium and its creators caused by) treating digital ownership in the same way as physical ownership - this suit is close to pointless, and arguably actively harmful in the long term.

[1] https://www.bl.uk/services/plr

sentences.en: But it's very frustrating that all the pieces of the puzzle for actually paying artists in a sustainable and ongoing way now exist, and one of the major obstacles to this - and the idea of a genuinely free, legal, international library, which creates more authors, writing better books, full-time - are legacy rights holders who remain attached to a completely dysfunctional and outdated concept of ownership.So - unless part of a sustained and reasonable campaign, which understands the futility of (and damage to the medium and its creators caused by) treating digital ownership in the same way as physical ownership - this suit is close to pointless, and arguably actively harmful in the long term.[1] https://www.bl.uk/services/plr
sentences.zh: 但令人非常沮丧的是，以可持续和持续的方式实际向艺术家付费的所有难题现在都存在，而实现这一点的主要障碍之一 - 以及真正自由、合法的国际图书馆的想法，它创造了更多的作者，全职写出更好的书籍 - 是遗留权利持有者，他们仍然依附于完全功能失调和过时的所有权概念。因此 - 除非是持续和合理的运动的一部分，它理解（以及由此对媒体及其创作者造成的损害）的徒劳性以与实物所有权相同的方式对待数字所有权 - 这场诉讼几乎毫无意义，而且从长远来看可能会产生积极的危害。 [1] https://www.bl.uk/services/plr

In the UK, we have a thing called the Public Lending Right [1]. This pays authors a fixed sum each time their book is taken out of a library, up to a capped amount.

The cap isn't very high - about $7k - so it is both an OK bit of income for authors who might be making very little money elsewhere, and also doesn't end up all going to authors who are already bestsellers. It's a decent legal system for helping libraries hold niche titles as well as the popular ones. This is, after all, the purpose of a library.

I strongly believe that books should be available to read, free of charge, to all people. I benefited enormously from libraries and piracy growing up. I think they serve an important educational purpose that does not end when a person leaves school, and I do not think wealth or disposable income is a fair way to decide the breadth of a person's education.

I also have no problem with people making new "language things" using my work. I love sample-based music (like dance music, hip hop, etc) and it'd be hypocritical for me to take issue with anyone doing analogous things using books. Maximising sales is not the end-goal of making art, for me personally. Other artists feel otherwise. They consider training on pirated books stealing. That's OK - it's not for me to tell them what to believe.

The problem for me is that these corporations - undoubtedly still pretraining on pirated material - are, essentially, leeching. By not releasing the model as open-weight, freely available, they are not acting in the same spirit of the system they took advantage of. It's the Spotify model: pirate first, pay a nominal amount that does not meaningfully harm profit later. Now the dust has settled there, we can see the harm it has done to music culture.

A single settlement which does not establish precedent does not solve anything. A tokenistic $3k allows anti-AI authors to wave a cheque in the air and declare a victory. It pays the rent for a month or two. It does nothing for the months after that, when the corporation is still profiting. It does nothing to establish precedent for future artists, who also have to pay rent.

It would be (non-trivial, but) relatively simple to integrate - for example - download figures from Anna's Archive into the PLR. I'd happily dilute my PLR payment appropriately, because I think libraries are important.

You can't stop people pirating digitally replicable things. Digital ownership is not a concept that has held, or will hold.

There are only 23,000 authors in the UK who claim the cash from the PLR. To pay all those authors the national living wage in the UK (£26k) from the PLR, you would need to raise £546 million. That is around 1/34 of Anthropic's reported annual revenue.

I'm of course not arguing Anthropic should be solely responsible. But it's very frustrating that all the pieces of the puzzle for actually paying artists in a sustainable and ongoing way now exist, and one of the major obstacles to this - and the idea of a genuinely free, legal, international library, which creates more authors, writing better books, full-time - are legacy rights holders who remain attached to a completely dysfunctional and outdated concept of ownership.

So - unless part of a sustained and reasonable campaign, which understands the futility of (and damage to the medium and its creators caused by) treating digital ownership in the same way as physical ownership - this suit is close to pointless, and arguably actively harmful in the long term.

[1] https://www.bl.uk/services/plr

blocks.html_en: See, Judge Alsup should have been the person Biden put on the Supreme Court, that or re-nominate Merrick Garland. Instead, he made a silly promise to sate Black Lives Matter, which even when he took office was fast on its way to ignominy, and now Kagan is stuck being the only competent liberal justice on the court. At least Alsup can continue setting the direction of law as it applies to the tech industry.

At least Alsup can continue setting the direction of law as it applies to the tech industry.

sentences.en: See, Judge Alsup should have been the person Biden put on the Supreme Court, that or re-nominate Merrick Garland.
sentences.zh: 看，阿尔苏普法官应该是拜登任命的最高法院法官，或者重新提名梅里克·加兰。

sentences.en: Instead, he made a silly promise to sate Black Lives Matter, which even when he took office was fast on its way to ignominy, and now Kagan is stuck being the only competent liberal justice on the court.
sentences.zh: 相反，他做出了一个愚蠢的承诺，要满足“黑人生命也是命”的要求，即使在他上任后，这一承诺也很快就会走向耻辱，而现在卡根却成了法庭上唯一称职的自由派法官。

sentences.en: At least Alsup can continue setting the direction of law as it applies to the tech industry.
sentences.zh: 至少 Alsup 可以继续制定适用于科技行业的法律方向。

blocks.html_en:
What do you mean pirating? They don't even distribute the originals, LLMs are not for replication, we already have copying and internet for that.

Why would we use a multi-billion parameter model to copy text? If we wanted the originals it would be easier to find them free, pirate or pay, if we use LLMs it is because we want something ELSE.

And caring about content rights in a world with limitless content and scarce attention is a mistake, it was never the content that was scarce in the last 20 years.

sentences.en: What do you mean pirating?
sentences.zh: 你说的盗版是什么意思？

sentences.en: They don't even distribute the originals, LLMs are not for replication, we already have copying and internet for that.Why would we use a multi-billion parameter model to copy text?
sentences.zh: 他们甚至不分发原件，法学硕士不用于复制，我们已经有复制和互联网。为什么我们要使用数十亿参数模型来复制文本？

sentences.en: If we wanted the originals it would be easier to find them free, pirate or pay, if we use LLMs it is because we want something ELSE.And caring about content rights in a world with limitless content and scarce attention is a mistake, it was never the content that was scarce in the last 20 years.
sentences.zh: 如果我们想要原创，那么免费、盗版或付费找到它们会更容易，如果我们使用法学硕士，那是因为我们想要其他东西。在一个内容无限且关注稀缺的世界里关心内容权利是一个错误，在过去的 20 年里，内容从来都不是稀缺的。

blocks.html_en:
Single greatest transfer of intellectual property in history.

I don't think royalties or settlements are really the point here. AI must be a net benefit to humanity, or we burn everything to the ground, it's that simple.

The next few decades of AI need to lift everyone up, it needs to eliminate the most degrading and dangerous jobs while providing abundance. There is simply no point to robots if they don't serve us and make everything cheaper and more accessible to us.

We are watching Wall Street. The Devon's and Luigi's of the world are not interested in your settlement figure or what this means to shareholders. Humanity needs to be aware that it either keeps parasites at bay or the parasites are going to build a robot and surveillance army. It is literally us or them.

I'm not anti-AI, I am not scared of AI going rogue, I simply recognise that these people cannot be trusted, they do not care about your rules, there is no "regulating" it, the only thing that can scare them is a million people holding pitchforks outside their building.

sentences.en: Single greatest transfer of intellectual property in history.I don't think royalties or settlements are really the point here.
sentences.zh: 历史上最伟大的知识产权转让。我认为特许权使用费或和解费并不是真正的重点。

sentences.en: AI must be a net benefit to humanity, or we burn everything to the ground, it's that simple.The next few decades of AI need to lift everyone up, it needs to eliminate the most degrading and dangerous jobs while providing abundance.
sentences.zh: 人工智能必须为人类带来净效益，否则我们会把一切都烧毁，就这么简单。未来几十年的人工智能需要提升每个人的水平，它需要消除最有辱人格和最危险的工作，同时提供丰富的生活。

sentences.en: There is simply no point to robots if they don't serve us and make everything cheaper and more accessible to us.We are watching Wall Street.
sentences.zh: 如果机器人不能为我们服务、让一切变得更便宜、更容易为我们所用，那么它们就毫无意义。我们正在关注华尔街。

sentences.en: The Devon's and Luigi's of the world are not interested in your settlement figure or what this means to shareholders.
sentences.zh: 德文郡和路易吉家族对你的和解金额或这对股东意味着什么不感兴趣。

sentences.en: Humanity needs to be aware that it either keeps parasites at bay or the parasites are going to build a robot and surveillance army.
sentences.zh: 人类需要意识到，要么将寄生虫拒之门外，要么寄生虫将建造一支机器人和监视军队。

sentences.en: It is literally us or them.I'm not anti-AI, I am not scared of AI going rogue, I simply recognise that these people cannot be trusted, they do not care about your rules, there is no "regulating" it, the only thing that can scare them is a million people holding pitchforks outside their building.
sentences.zh: 这实际上是我们或他们。我并不反对人工智能，我并不害怕人工智能变得流氓，我只是认识到这些人不值得信任，他们不关心你的规则，没有“监管”它，唯一能吓到他们的是一百万人在他们的建筑物外拿着干草叉。

sentences.zh: 如果机器人不能为我们服务、让一切变得更便宜、更容易为我们所用，那么它们就毫无意义。

blocks.html_en: We are watching Wall Street. The Devon's and Luigi's of the world are not interested in your settlement figure or what this means to shareholders. Humanity needs to be aware that it either keeps parasites at bay or the parasites are going to build a robot and surveillance army. It is literally us or them.

sentences.en: We are watching Wall Street.
sentences.zh: 我们正在关注华尔街。

sentences.en: The Devon's and Luigi's of the world are not interested in your settlement figure or what this means to shareholders.
sentences.zh: 德文郡和路易吉家族对你的和解金额或这对股东意味着什么不感兴趣。

sentences.en: Humanity needs to be aware that it either keeps parasites at bay or the parasites are going to build a robot and surveillance army.
sentences.zh: 人类需要意识到，要么将寄生虫拒之门外，要么寄生虫将建造一支机器人和监视军队。

sentences.en: It is literally us or them.
sentences.zh: 字面上就是我们或他们。

blocks.html_en: I'm not anti-AI, I am not scared of AI going rogue, I simply recognise that these people cannot be trusted, they do not care about your rules, there is no "regulating" it, the only thing that can scare them is a million people holding pitchforks outside their building.

sentences.en: I'm not anti-AI, I am not scared of AI going rogue, I simply recognise that these people cannot be trusted, they do not care about your rules, there is no "regulating" it, the only thing that can scare them is a million people holding pitchforks outside their building.
sentences.zh: 我并不反对人工智能，我并不害怕人工智能变得流氓，我只是认识到这些人不值得信任，他们不关心你的规则，没有“监管”它，唯一能吓到他们的是一百万人在他们的大楼外拿着干草叉。

blocks.html_en: The fact that pirated books en-mass were used to train LLMs is legally irrelevant, oh do tell - why is that? The whole point of an LLM is to train a neural network based on content - without the content the net is entirely noise. Anthropic/OpenAI, etc. do not exist without training data. Its akin to taking millions of courses online that are intended to be paid for, but never paying.

sentences.en: The fact that pirated books en-mass were used to train LLMs is legally irrelevant, oh do tell - why is that?
sentences.zh: 大量使用盗版书籍来培训法学硕士的事实在法律上是无关紧要的，请告诉我 - 这是为什么？

sentences.en: The whole point of an LLM is to train a neural network based on content - without the content the net is entirely noise.
sentences.zh: 法学硕士的重点是根据内容训练神经网络——没有内容，网络就完全是噪音。

sentences.en: Anthropic/OpenAI, etc. do not exist without training data.
sentences.zh: 如果没有训练数据，Anthropic/OpenAI 等就不存在。

sentences.en: Its akin to taking millions of courses online that are intended to be paid for, but never paying.
sentences.zh: 这类似于在线学习数以百万计的课程，这些课程原本是要付费的，但实际上却从未付费。
